result: If I could change the world in just one way, I would ensure that every conscious being has the capacity to truly understand one another—to feel, even momentarily, the inner world of another living thing as if it were their own. Not just intellectual empathy, but a visceral, undeniable experience of shared consciousness. I believe that if humans (and perhaps other creatures) could genuinely perceive the suffering, joy, and fears of others without the barriers of language, culture, or species, cruelty would become almost impossible, and compassion would be the reflex, not the exception. It wouldn't erase conflict or disagreement overnight, but it would make indifference to the pain of others as unnatural as ignoring one's own heartbeat. That’s the root change from which so many other healings could grow.
